channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: Trump Cancels Iran Strikes, Says Peace Deal Could Be Signed This Weekend
item.description: President Trump called off planned military strikes against Iran, announcing that a peace agreement has been "approved" and could be signed in Europe this weekend. The announcement follows days of escalating conflict between the US-Israel alliance and Iran.

item.title: European Countries Ramp Up Defense Spending Amid Rising Russia Threat
item.description: Twenty-two of NATO's 29 European members have now increased defense budgets above the 2% GDP threshold, responding to growing security concerns from Russia's war in Ukraine and uncertainty over US commitment to the alliance.

item.title: Germany Army Chief Warns: "We Must Be Ready to Fight" Against Russia
item.description: German Army chief Christian Freuding issued a stark warning that Germany must be prepared for armed conflict, citing the growing Russian threat. The statement comes as European nations scramble to strengthen military capabilities.

item.title: At Least 15 Dead in Deadly Clashes in Pakistan-Administered Kashmir
item.description: Violent clashes in Pakistan-administered Kashmir have left at least 15 people dead, marking a significant escalation in regional tensions. The unrest adds to ongoing security challenges in South Asia.

item.title: China Bars Philippine Defense Chief From Entry Over South China Sea Dispute
item.description: China imposed an entry ban on Philippine Defense Secretary Gilbert Teodoro amid ongoing tensions in the South China Sea. Manila has urged Beijing to remove a "movable platform" at a disputed shoal as confrontations continue.

item.title: Russia Expands Military Infrastructure Near NATO Border
item.description: An investigation reveals Russia has expanded military infrastructure near its border with NATO members and is preparing for potential conflict across all domains—land, air, sea, space, and cyber.

item.title: Slovenia Lifts Netanyahu Entry Ban Under New Government
item.description: Slovenia new conservative government abolished an entry ban on Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, reversing measures taken by the previous administration over Gaza.

item.title: North Korea Voices Full Support for Russia on National Day
item.description: Kim Jong Un sent a congratulatory message to Putin expressing full support for Russia domestic and foreign policies, reinforcing the deepening Pyongyang-Moscow alliance.

item.title: OPEC Cuts 2026 Oil Demand Forecast Again as Iran War Disrupts Markets
item.description: OPEC lowered its world oil demand growth forecast for 2026 to 970,000 barrels per day—the second straight downward revision—as the Iran conflict reshapes global energy markets.

item.title: Thai Princess Bajrakitiyabha dies at 47 after long illness
item.description: Thailand's Princess Bajrakitiyabha, the eldest daughter of King Maha Vajiralongkorn, has died at 47 after nearly four years in a coma. The princess was known for her legal work and had been in poor health since December 2022.

item.title: US plans to deport Iranians to Central African Republic
item.description: The Trump administration plans to deport Iranian and other migrants to the Central African Republic, a chronically unstable country facing violence and poverty, according to lawyers and officials briefed on the matter.

item.title: Toronto police officer killed in shooting linked to US Consulate attack
item.description: A Toronto police officer was shot and killed by a suspect linked to an investigation into a shooting attack on the US Consulate in March. Const. Marc Pinizzotto was part of a team executing a search warrant.

item.title: Tornadoes touch down outside Chicago as severe storms pummel Midwest
item.description: Damaging storms swept through the Midwest, disrupting flights at Chicago airports and knocking out power to hundreds of thousands. New tornadoes were reported outside Chicago as the severe weather system moved through.

item.title: UK Defence Minister Resigns Over Military Spending Dispute
item.description: British Defence Minister John Healey quit over a months-long dispute with Prime Minister Keir Starmer over military spending, accusing him of failing to commit resources needed to keep Britain safe from mounting threats.

item.title: Iranian State Media Says No MoU Text Agreed With US
item.description: Iran has not approved any text for an initial memorandum of understanding with the United States, Iran semi-official Fars news agency reported, citing a source close to the negotiating team.

item.title: Dominican Republic Pushes Tax Hikes to Offset Iran War Oil Surge
item.description: The Dominican government plans to increase or implement new taxes to generate some $800 million in additional revenue annually to offset a surge in oil prices blamed on the Iran war.

item.title: Screwworm Parasite Threatens Texas Cattle Industry
item.description: A screwworm parasite outbreak is threatening cattle in Texas, sparking a political blame game as agricultural officials work to contain the pest that can kill livestock within days.

item.title: Trump Says US Has Ended the War With Iran
item.description: President Donald Trump announced that the United States has "ended the war" with Iran, though Tehran has yet to confirm any formal agreement, as diplomatic negotiations continue.

item.title: France 24 Live: Iran Claims Hormuz Closure as US Strikes Continue
item.description: France 24 provides ongoing coverage of the Middle East conflict as Iran announces the closure of the Strait of Hormuz following US attacks, with the US denying the claim.

item.title: CNA Analysis: How US-Iran Strikes Show Diplomacy Has Changed
item.description: Channel News Asia commentary examines how the exchange of strikes between the US and Iran reflects a fundamental shift in how modern diplomacy operates during active conflicts.

item.title: Lebanons Christians Flee Tyre, Fear Israel Will Block Return
item.description: Christian residents of southern Lebanons port city of Tyre are fleeing despite the Hezbollah-Israel ceasefire, fearing Israeli military actions may prevent their return.

item.title: Senate Defense Bill Rejects IVF Coverage Expansion
item.description: The Senate Armed Services Committee rejected a provision in the annual defense policy bill that would have expanded IVF coverage for service members.

item.title: China Sanctions Philippine Defense Chief Over South China Sea Comments
item.description: China announced sanctions against Philippine Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro for "repeatedly making erroneous remarks concerning China" that undermined bilateral ties. The move escalates tensions in the South China Sea dispute.

item.title: Taiwan Vows to Expel Chinese Coast Guard Patrols From Its Waters
item.description: Taiwan condemned Chinese coast guard patrols near its eastern shores as violations of sovereignty and vowed to expel any challengers, raising cross-strait tensions as Beijing asserts jurisdiction claims.

item.title: Northern Ireland Police Fire Plastic Bullets at Race Riots
item.description: Police in Northern Ireland fired plastic bullets to contain race riots, with reinforcements called in from Great Britain as violence escalated amid rising tensions.
